# Nicole Bradbury

A professional artist seeking an online presence to showcase her paintings and artwork. The website will serve as a digital gallery featuring her work with individual painting descriptions, an about section sharing her story and background, pricing information for collectors and buyers, and contact details for inquiries and commissions.

Source: https://example.com

---

## Discover Original Art at This Fine Arts Center — Nicole Bradbury

URL: https://example.com
Description: Step inside Nicole Bradbury&#39;s digital fine arts center and explore a breathtaking collection of original paintings. Find your next statement piece and bring fine art home today.

# Paintings That Hold the Light


### Nicole Bradbury creates original works that capture the quiet atmosphere of coastal landscapes and the intimate energy of the studio process.


View the Collection
Commission a Painting













## Featured Works



A curated selection of recent paintings, each capturing a quiet moment of light and texture. Explore the collection to discover the stories behind the canvas.













### Tuscan Light


2024 · Oil on Linen · 36″ × 48″


A study of afternoon light as it spills across the Val d'Orcia, where shadows stretch long and the air itself seems to hold the scent of warm earth.


View in Gallery →










### Quiet Offering


2024 · Acrylic on Canvas · 18″ × 24″


A meditation on stillness and simplicity, celebrating the humble beauty of dried botanicals against time-worn plaster.


View in Gallery →










### Coastal Reverie


2023 · Oil on Panel · 20″ × 30″


Capturing the raw energy of the Atlantic coast, where water meets stone in a timeless, rhythmic dialogue.


View in Gallery →










### Bloom Unfolding


2024 · Mixed Media on Canvas · 40″ × 40″


An exploration of growth and renewal, built through layers of translucent washes and thick impasto petals that seem to breathe on the canvas.


View in Gallery →





















## The Art of Quiet Observation


A Journey Through Texture, Light, and Memory




Nicole Bradbury is a contemporary fine artist whose work explores the delicate interplay between natural light and the forgotten corners of domestic spaces. Drawing inspiration from the muted palettes of the English countryside and the weathered textures of coastal studios, her paintings invite viewers into a world of quiet contemplation.


With a background in classical oil techniques and a deep appreciation for modernist composition, Nicole's process is one of patient layering. Each canvas is built over weeks, allowing glazes to settle and forms to emerge organically. Her work is held in private collections across Europe and North America, and she has been featured in several national exhibitions celebrating the resurgence of representational painting.





“I don't seek to capture a moment, but rather the feeling of having lived within it. My paintings are invitations to pause, to breathe, and to see the extraordinary in the ordinary.”




Read the Full Story



























## Collecting & Pricing


Every piece begins with a conversation. Below are general starting ranges to help you envision a work in your space. Final pricing is determined by size, medium, and framing choices.









### Small Works


Intimate studies, sketches, and smaller canvases (up to 12×16 in). Perfect for starting a collection or adding a quiet moment to a bookshelf or hallway.


$400 – $1,200








### Medium Works


Statement pieces for living areas and dining rooms (up to 30×40 in). These works balance presence with intimacy and often become the heart of a room.


$1,500 – $4,800








### Statement Pieces


Large-scale commissioned works (36×48 in and above) designed to anchor a space. These are collaborative journeys from initial concept to final installation.


$5,200 – $12,000+






Commissions are welcomed year-round. Each inquiry includes a studio visit or virtual consultation, material samples, and framing guidance. Shipping and installation can be arranged for collectors worldwide.

Inquire About a Piece























## Begin a Conversation






For collectors, interior designers, and lovers of fine art — I welcome your inquiries about available works, commissioned pieces, or simply a conversation about the studio process.


Inquire About Works
Learn My Story  →




















Quiet compositions, considered light. Original fine art for collectors and spaces that value stillness.

# A Curated Portfolio of Original Paintings


Welcome to the digital studio of Nicole Bradbury. Each piece in this collection is an original work, carrying its own distinct mood and narrative. From still lifes that capture quiet moments to landscapes that breathe with atmosphere, every painting reflects a personal journey of observation and expression. Browse the gallery to discover works available for collectors and commissions.


View the Collection  →


















## The Collection





Each piece is a conversation between light and texture, captured on canvas in my studio overlooking the forest hills. Explore the works below and find a story that speaks to you.













### Whispering Pines


Oil on Canvas
·
36″ × 48″
·
2024



Inspired by early morning walks through the forest hills fine arts center grounds, this piece captures the quiet moment before the world wakes. The layered brushwork creates depth that shifts with the light throughout the day.


Available











### Canyon Echo


Acrylic on Canvas
·
48″ × 60″
·
2023



A bold exploration of movement and memory, this large-scale work draws from the eroded landscapes of the Southwest. The palette balances warmth and restraint, making it a commanding presence in any collection.


Sold











### Morning Stillness


Oil on Linen
·
24″ × 30″
·
2024



A return to the quiet discipline of still life, this painting celebrates the fleeting beauty of wildflowers gathered from the garden. The soft window light creates a gentle narrative of domestic tranquility.


Available











### Tidal Memory


Oil on Canvas
·
40″ × 40″
·
2023



Painted during a residency on the northern coast, this square-format seascape channels the raw energy of an approaching squall. The textured impasto technique gives the waves a sculptural, almost tangible quality.


Available











### The Reader


Oil on Panel
·
30″ × 24″
·
2022



An intimate portrait of my daughter lost in a novel, bathed in the amber light of a late afternoon. This piece is part of an ongoing series exploring quiet domestic rituals and the beauty of ordinary moments.


Sold











### Meadow Song


Acrylic on Canvas
·
36″ × 48″
·
2024



A celebration of high summer captured en plein air over three successive afternoons. The rapid, joyful brushwork reflects the buzzing energy of the meadow, alive with wildflowers and the hum of pollinators.


Available











### Horizon Line No. 3


Mixed Media on Canvas
·
48″ × 36″
·
2024



Part of a meditative series exploring the intersection of earth and sky. Layer upon layer of translucent wash creates an atmospheric depth, punctuated by a single, decisive line that anchors the composition.


Available











### Copper & Peony


Oil on Linen
·
30″ × 40″
·
2023



A contemporary homage to the Dutch Golden Age, this painting luxuriates in the velvety texture of peony petals and the warm gleam of polished copper. The dark background pushes the blooms forward with dramatic intensity.


Sold











### Vesper Light


Oil on Canvas
·
24″ × 36″
·
2024



The final glow of dusk captured in soft, seamless gradients. This piece is about transition — the threshold between day and night, consciousness and dreams. A quiet, contemplative work for a serene space.


Available









Interested in a custom piece? Inquire about commissions for works tailored to your space and vision.










## The Studio Process



Materials, mark making, and the quiet rhythm of the studio.










### Sketching & Composition


Every painting begins with a quiet conversation between pencil and paper. Nicole works in natural light, allowing the shifting shadows to guide her initial marks. These early sketches are not rigid blueprints but fluid explorations of balance, movement, and emotional weight.










### Layering & Pigment


Using a restrained palette of earth tones and muted hues, Nicole builds her surfaces slowly. Thin washes give way to thicker impasto passages, creating a tactile depth that invites the viewer to look closer. Each layer holds the memory of the one beneath it.










### Texture & Final Details


The final stage is a meditation on texture. Nicole sands, scrapes, and reworks the surface until it feels alive. The finished painting carries the evidence of its making — subtle ridges, soft patinas, and a quiet luminosity that changes with the light in the room.













## Availability & Inquiries



A practical guide for collectors interested in acquiring original work or commissioning a new piece.





### Current Inventory


Most paintings shown in the gallery are available for immediate purchase unless marked as sold. I update the collection regularly, but please reach out if you have your eye on a specific piece — I’ll confirm its status and send you detailed images, dimensions, and pricing within 24 hours.


For collectors visiting the fine arts center exhibitions, I can also arrange private viewings of works not currently displayed online.




### Commissions & Framing


I welcome a limited number of commissions each season. Lead times typically range from six to ten weeks depending on scale and complexity. We’ll discuss your vision, preferred palette, and dimensions before I begin.


All paintings are delivered ready to hang with hand-selected, archival-quality framing. I personally oversee every step from studio to crate to ensure your artwork arrives safely and beautifully presented.

Request a commission consultation  →













Quiet compositions, considered light. Original fine art for collectors and spaces that value stillness.

## Meet the Vision Behind the Forest Hills Fine Arts Center — Nicole Bradbury

URL: https://example.com/about
Description: Uncover the story, passion, and creative journey of artist Nicole Bradbury. Learn what drives her brush and how her work elevates the contemporary fine arts scene.

Meet the Vision Behind the Forest Hills Fine Arts Center — Nicole Bradbury






























Skip to main content















- Home
- Gallery
- About
- Collectors & Pricing
- Contact



Contact























# Nicole Bradbury



I'm a fine artist working from a sunlit studio in the countryside, where the shifting seasons and quiet landscapes find their way into every canvas. My work lives at the intersection of observation and emotion — each painting begins with something I've seen and ends with something I've felt.


My path into fine arts wasn't a straight line. I studied literature before I ever picked up a brush seriously, and those years of reading — of paying attention to rhythm, texture, and the weight of a single detail — still shape how I compose a painting. After formal training at a small atelier and years of independent practice, I began exhibiting in local galleries and eventually opened my own studio. Today, my work is held in private collections across the country, and I continue to explore the dialogue between realism and abstraction.


### What Painting Means to Me


"Painting is how I slow the world down. When I stand before a blank canvas, everything external falls away — the noise, the hurry, the endless scroll. What remains is color and light and the quiet urgency of making something true. Every brushstroke is a conversation between what I see and what I remember, and my hope is that someone standing before the finished work feels that same stillness I felt while creating it."







In the studio, 2024












## The Journey Behind the Canvas













2008

### Foundations in Fine Arts


Nicole graduated with a Bachelor of Fine Arts from the Rhode Island School of Design, where she developed her foundational skills in oil painting and mixed media. Immersed in a rigorous studio environment, she began exploring the interplay between light and texture that would later define her signature style.










2012

### A Turning Point in Tuscany


After a transformative residency in the hills of Tuscany, Nicole shifted her focus toward capturing fleeting moments of natural beauty. The golden Italian light and weathered landscapes inspired a new series of impressionistic works, marking a profound evolution in her artistic voice and emotional depth.










2016

### Solo Exhibition at the National Museum of Fine Arts


Nicole's first major solo show, "Whispers of Light," debuted at the National Museum of Fine Arts. The exhibition featured over thirty large-scale canvases and was met with critical acclaim, establishing her as a rising voice in contemporary fine arts and drawing the attention of collectors nationwide.










2020

### Exploring Abstraction and Emotion


During a period of global quietude, Nicole retreated to her forest hills studio to experiment with abstraction. Moving beyond literal landscapes, she began creating visceral, large-format pieces that translate emotional states into sweeping color fields and layered textures, resonating deeply with a new audience seeking connection through art.










Present

### A Living Studio Practice


Today, Nicole works from her sun-drenched studio, balancing commissioned portraits with an ongoing exploration of natural forms. Her current practice bridges traditional techniques with a modern sensibility, welcoming collectors and art lovers into a calm, personal dialogue with each piece she creates.














## Core Artistic Values





What defines the work beyond subject matter alone — a philosophy rooted in atmosphere, material sensitivity, and emotional resonance.






### Atmosphere


Every piece begins with a quiet intention — a soft, enveloping mood that invites the viewer to linger. Nicole's work is less about capturing a literal scene and more about evoking the air, the light, and the stillness that transforms a canvas into a sanctuary. This atmospheric quality is the foundation of her fine arts center of practice.





### Material Sensitivity


A deep reverence for the physical substance of art guides every brushstroke. From the tooth of handmade paper to the weight of oil pigment, Nicole treats her materials as collaborators. This tactile dialogue between artist and medium creates a richness that collectors can feel, even from across a room, making each work a testament to the craft behind fine art.





### Emotional Resonance


Beyond technique, Nicole seeks a genuine emotional connection — an unspoken conversation between the painting and its viewer. Her compositions hold space for memory, longing, and quiet joy, creating a personal experience that lingers long after the gallery visit ends. It is this resonance that turns a painting into a cherished companion within a home.













## Collector Impressions











Nicole's work carries an emotional weight that transforms any room. Her landscapes don't just hang on the wall — they breathe. Every collector who visits my home pauses longest in front of her pieces.



Eleanor Voss
Interior Designer, Voss & Co.









There is a rare craftsmanship in every brushstroke. The way light touches the canvas in her still-life series reminds me why I fell in love with fine arts in the first place. A true gem in the contemporary art scene.



Marcus Chen
Curator, National Museum of Fine Arts Affiliate
















Quiet compositions, considered light. Original fine art for collectors and spaces that value stillness.

## Invest in Masterpieces From a National Museum of Fine Arts Talent — Nicole Bradbury

URL: https://example.com/collectors-pricing
Description: Exclusive pricing and acquisition details for serious collectors seeking investment-worthy fine art. Secure a timeless Nicole Bradbury original before it&#39;s gone forever.

Invest in Masterpieces From a National Museum of Fine Arts Talent — Nicole Bradbury






























Skip to main content















- Home
- Gallery
- About
- Collectors & Pricing
- Contact



Contact










# Acquiring Original Work



Nicole believes that acquiring art should feel personal and unhurried. Each piece is priced according to its size, medium, and the time invested in the studio, with complete transparency from the very first conversation. Whether you are drawn to a finished gallery piece or are curious about commissioning something entirely new, Nicole welcomes your inquiry. Availability changes with the seasons, and she is always happy to share what is currently on the easel.


Below you will find a general guide to pricing ranges for original paintings. For exact pricing on a specific work, or to discuss a custom commission, please reach out directly. Nicole looks forward to connecting with you.















## Pricing & Collection Guidance






Every painting is an original work, priced by scale, complexity, and the story it carries. Below you'll find approximate ranges to guide your collecting journey.







Small Works


### Intimate Studies



Up to 12" × 16" (30 × 40 cm)


$
400
–
$
1,200


Perfect for first-time collectors or adding a subtle accent to a curated shelf or reading nook. These pieces often capture fleeting light and quiet moments.


Inquire about small works







Medium Works


### Signature Pieces



16" × 20" to 30" × 40" (40 × 50 cm to 76 × 102 cm)


$
1,500
–
$
4,800


The heart of most collections. These works command presence above a mantle, console, or dining table, balancing narrative depth with generous scale.


Inquire about medium works







Large Works


### Statement Commissions



36" × 48" and larger (91 × 122 cm+)


$
5,200
–
$
18,000
+


Designed for grand entries, double-height walls, and institutional spaces. Each large-scale work begins with a studio visit and a conversation about your vision.


Inquire about large works






Prices reflect unframed works on canvas or panel. Framing, crating, and shipping are available upon request. For a tailored proposal or to visit the studio, please get in touch.









## The Collector's Experience






### Original Artwork & Authenticity


Every painting is an original work, signed and accompanied by a certificate of authenticity. I document the materials, palette, and story behind each piece so your collection carries a complete provenance from studio to home. This is more than a purchase — it is the beginning of a relationship with the artwork.





### Care & Preservation Guidance


Oil paintings reward thoughtful stewardship, and I want your piece to age beautifully for generations. With every acquisition, I share detailed care recommendations — from ideal lighting and humidity levels to gentle cleaning — so you can feel confident displaying your painting in a cherished space within your home.





### Commission Collaboration


A commissioned painting is a deeply personal endeavor. I work closely with you from initial concept sketches through final varnish, ensuring the scale, palette, and emotional tone align with your vision. Clear timelines, transparent pricing, and gentle communication make the process as rewarding as the finished canvas.





















## Collecting & Commissioning



Answers to the most common questions about acquiring, caring for, and commissioning original artwork. If you don't find what you're looking for, please reach out directly—I'm always happy to talk about the process.





### How do I reserve a painting I'm interested in?


Once you've found a piece you love, simply get in touch via the contact form or email. I can place a 48-hour courtesy hold while we finalize details. To secure the work, a 30% deposit is required, with the remaining balance due prior to shipping or collection.




### What does the commission process look like?


Commissions begin with a conversation about size, palette, and the feeling you'd like the piece to convey. After we agree on a direction, I provide a sketch and a timeline. A 40% non-refundable deposit initiates the work, with the balance settled upon your approval of the finished painting.




### Do you ship internationally?


Yes, I ship to collectors throughout the United States, Canada, the UK, and the European Union. Each painting is carefully packed in a custom crate with full insurance coverage. Shipping costs are calculated based on size and destination and will be quoted at the time of purchase.




### Are framing options available?


Most works on canvas arrive ready to hang with gallery-wrapped edges, so framing is optional. For works on paper, I collaborate with a fine arts framer who offers a curated selection of hand-finished mouldings. I'm pleased to guide you through those choices.




### How should I care for an original painting?


Display your artwork away from direct sunlight, heat sources, and areas of high humidity. Dust gently with a soft, dry microfiber cloth—never use cleaning products. With proper placement and occasional light dusting, your painting will remain vibrant for generations.




### What is your typical timeline for a new work?


Available studio pieces ship within 5–7 business days. Commissioned paintings generally require 4–8 weeks depending on scale and complexity. I'll provide a firm delivery window once we've defined the scope of your project, and I keep collectors updated with progress images along the way.














Quiet compositions, considered light. Original fine art for collectors and spaces that value stillness.

# Let's Start a Conversation


I welcome artwork inquiries, commission requests, collector questions, and collaborations with interior designers. Whether you're drawn to a specific piece or envision something entirely new, I'd love to hear from you.




























## Inquiries & Commissions


I welcome questions about available paintings, custom commissions, and studio visits. Please fill in the form and I will reply within 48 hours.




Full Name




Email Address




Phone Number




Your Message






I have read and agree to the  Terms & Conditions  and  Privacy Policy



Send Inquiry







### Contact Nicole



- [business_email]


- [business_phone]


- [business_address]






I typically respond within 48 hours. For urgent inquiries, please call the studio directly.













## Commission a Painting



I welcome commissions that allow us to create something deeply personal. Here's what to expect when we collaborate on a custom artwork.










### Commission Themes


My commissioned work often explores intimate landscapes, botanical studies, and quiet interior moments. We'll work together to define a subject that resonates with your space and story, whether it's a cherished place or a personal still life.









### The Process


After our initial conversation, I'll prepare a series of small compositional sketches and a shared mood board. Once we've settled on a direction, I'll begin the painting, sharing progress updates so you can see the piece evolve naturally.









### Timeline & Expectations


Most commissions take between 4 to 8 weeks, depending on scale and complexity. I'll provide a clear timeline and pricing proposal before we begin, and I'm always happy to discuss framing and delivery options for your finished piece.




















## The Studio


Forest Hills, New York


Nicole works from her light-filled studio in Forest Hills, a quiet neighborhood that has long been home to artists and creatives. The space is not a public gallery—it is a working studio where paintings are conceived, layered, and brought to completion.


Private viewings are available by appointment for serious collectors and those interested in commissioning a new work. Please reach out through the contact form to arrange a visit.


Inquire About a Visit
→
















Quiet compositions, considered light. Original fine art for collectors and spaces that value stillness.

## Privacy Policy

Description: Our privacy policy - how we collect, use, and protect your information

- Home

Contact








# Privacy Policy



Last Updated:  6/20/2026



Nicole Bradbury ("Company", "we", "our") is committed to protecting your privacy. This privacy policy explains how we collect, use and protect your information when you use our website [business_website] ("Service").


## 1. Information Collection


We may collect the following types of information:


- Personal Information: Name, email address, phone number and address

- Technical Information: IP address, browser type, operating system and website usage data

- Cookies: Information stored on your computer to improve user experience




## 2. Use of Information


We use the collected information for the following purposes:


- Providing requested services

- Improving service and user experience

- Contacting you regarding our services

- Compliance with legal requirements




## 3. Information Sharing


We do not sell, rent or share your personal information with third parties, except in the following cases:


- With your explicit consent

- When required by law

- To protect our rights or user safety

- With service providers who assist in operating the service (under confidentiality agreements)




## 4. User Rights


In accordance with Israeli Privacy Protection Law 1981, you have the following rights:


- Right to access personal information stored about you

- Right to correct inaccurate or incomplete information

- Right to delete personal information in certain circumstances

- Right to object to information processing for marketing purposes

- Right to file a complaint with the Data Protection Authority




## 5. Cookies


Our website uses cookies to improve user experience. You can manage cookies through your browser settings. Disabling cookies may affect website functionality.


## 6. Information Security


We implement reasonable security measures to protect your information, including encryption, firewalls and restricted access control. However, no transmission or storage method is 100% secure.


## 7. Policy Changes


We may update this privacy policy from time to time. Material changes will be posted on the website with 30 days advance notice.



## 8. Contact Information


For questions or requests regarding this privacy policy, please contact us:


- Company: Nicole Bradbury

- Email: [business_email]

- Phone: [business_phone]

- Address: [business_address]



Data Protection Authority: For complaints, you may contact the Data Protection Authority at the Ministry of Justice

## Accessibility Statement

Description: Our accessibility statement - our commitment to digital accessibility

- Home

Contact








# Accessibility Statement



Last updated:  June 20, 2026



Nicole Bradbury is committed to making our services accessible to all users. We strive to implement the Israeli Standard (TI 5568) based on WCAG 2.1 Level AA guidelines.


Accessibility Level: AA (Israeli Standard TI 5568)



## 1. Accessibility Policy


We believe in equal rights and the right of every person to receive information and services independently and equally. This website was designed and built with special emphasis on accessibility for people with disabilities.


## 2. Accessibility Arrangements on the Site


- Keyboard navigation support

- Screen reader compatibility

- Appropriate color contrast

- Flexible and scalable font sizes

- Descriptive image alt text

- Hierarchical heading structure

- Clear and descriptive links

- Support for 200% zoom without content loss





### WCAG 2.1 Compliance


The website complies with WCAG 2.1 Level AA, which includes:


- Perceivable: Information is presented in ways users can perceive

- Operable: Interface components are operable

- Understandable: Information and user interface are understandable

- Robust: Content can be interpreted by a variety of technologies





## 3. Supported Assistive Technologies


- NVDA (Windows)

- JAWS (Windows)

- VoiceOver (Mac/iOS)

- TalkBack (Android)

- Dragon NaturallySpeaking

- Switch navigation




## 4. Accessibility Testing


The website has been tested using:


- Automated accessibility testing tools

- Manual testing with screen readers

- Keyboard-only navigation

- Color contrast verification

- Testing with users with disabilities




## 5. Continuous Improvements


We are continuously working to improve the accessibility of our website and update it according to technological developments and user feedback.



## 6. Contact and Feedback


If you encounter an accessibility issue on the site or need assistance, please contact us:


- Company: Nicole Bradbury

- Email: [business_email]

- Phone: [business_phone]



Response time: We respond to accessibility inquiries within 3 business days and work to resolve issues within 10 business days.

# Terms of Use



Last updated:  June 20, 2026



Use of this site constitutes agreement to the terms outlined below. Please read carefully before use.



## 1. Definitions


- "Company" - Nicole Bradbury

- "User" - Anyone who browses or uses the site

- "Services" - Fine Arts provided through the site




## 2. Description of Services


Nicole Bradbury provides Fine Arts through the site. The services are subject to the terms detailed in this document and may change from time to time.


## 3. Terms of Use


Use of the site is conditional on compliance with the following terms:


- Use of the site for lawful purposes only

- No interference with site operations or other users

- No uploading of offensive, illegal, or harmful content

- Respect for copyright and intellectual property rights

- No actions that may compromise site security




## 4. User Obligations


- Providing accurate and truthful information during registration or ordering

- Maintaining confidentiality of account access details

- Immediate notification of any unauthorized account use

- Updating personal information as needed




## 5. Payment and Refund Policy


If services include payment:


- Payments are processed through secure and encrypted systems

- Prices include applicable taxes as required by law

- Right to cancel will be in accordance with consumer protection laws

- Refunds will be processed within 14 business days of cancellation approval




## 6. Limitation of Liability


Services are provided "as is" without warranty of any kind. The Company will not be liable for indirect, consequential, or special damages arising from use of the site, except where required by law.


## 7. Copyright and Intellectual Property


All content on the site, including text, images, design, and code, is protected by copyright owned by Nicole Bradbury. Reproduction, distribution, or commercial use of content is prohibited without written permission.


## 8. Privacy


Collection and use of personal information is subject to our Privacy Policy. Use of the site constitutes agreement to the Privacy Policy as detailed separately.


## 9. Changes to Terms of Use


The Company may change these terms of use at any time. Material changes will be posted on the site with 30 days advance notice. Continued use after changes constitutes agreement to the new terms.


## 10. Service Termination


The Company may suspend or limit service at any time, including in cases of terms of use violations. In case of service termination, relevant provisions will remain in effect.


## 11. Governing Law and Dispute Resolution


These terms of use are governed by applicable laws. Any disputes will be resolved in accordance with local jurisdiction, with preference given to arbitration or mediation where possible.


## 12. General Provisions


- If any portion of the terms is found invalid, remaining terms will remain in effect

- Non-enforcement of a right will not be considered a waiver

- These terms of use constitute the entire agreement between the parties





## 13. Contact Information


For questions or clarifications regarding the terms of use:


- Company: Nicole Bradbury

- Email: [business_email]

- Phone: [business_phone]

- Address: [business_address]
